topics.label: Route and security concern
topics.answer: If routing through Dubai already makes you anxious, I would not build the trip around that connection. Even when flights are operating, peace of mind is part of a good itinerary. Better alternatives include Turkish Airlines via Istanbul, Ethiopian Airlines via Addis Ababa, Kenya Airways via Nairobi, or direct and seasonal Frankfurt services when the dates match. Compare the calmer options by total travel time, connection quality, and price difference. If a route costs moderately more but removes a concern that would follow you from booking until departure, the extra cost may be worthwhile. A comfortable decision should still feel sensible when you review it a few days later. Security conditions and airline operations can change quickly, so verify current official travel advisories and airline updates before booking.
facts: Alternative connections include Istanbul, Addis Ababa, and Nairobi.
Direct or seasonal Frankfurt services may be available when dates match.
Security conditions and airline operations require current verification.

ayor_notes: Travel planning is not only about price and duration. If a route feels stressful before booking, that is already a valid reason to choose another connection.

topics.label: Flights from Germany
topics.answer:
From Germany, AYOR identifies Frankfurt as the direct-flight departure point. Condor is listed with a Friday outbound and Saturday inbound service, resuming on October 9, 2026. Discover Airlines is listed with outbound flights on Mondays and Wednesdays and inbound flights on Tuesdays and Sundays, resuming on June 26, 2026.

If those dates or departure days do not fit, the relevant one-stop options are Turkish Airlines via Istanbul, Ethiopian Airlines via Addis Ababa, and Kenya Airways via Nairobi. All international services arrive at Seychelles International Airport, SEZ, on Mahé.

Decision: compare the Frankfurt direct options first for your exact dates, then compare one-stop routes by total journey time and connection quality. Because flight schedules are time-sensitive, verify the operating dates with the airline before booking. If you plan to continue to Praslin after arrival, leave enough time for immigration, baggage collection, and the onward connection.
facts: Condor: direct from Frankfurt; outbound Friday; inbound Saturday; resumes October 9, 2026.
Discover Airlines: direct from Frankfurt; outbound Monday and Wednesday; inbound Tuesday and Sunday; resumes June 26, 2026.
One-stop alternatives: Turkish Airlines via Istanbul, Ethiopian Airlines via Addis Ababa, Kenya Airways via Nairobi.
International arrival airport: SEZ on Mahé.

If your final destination is Praslin or La Digue, treat the onward island movement as a separate connection rather than part of the international arrival.

topics.label: Flights from Europe
topics.answer:
AYOR lists several practical European routes to Mahé. Frankfurt has direct services from Condor and Discover Airlines on specified seasonal schedules. Zurich has a direct Edelweiss Air service, with no flights listed between May 30 and September 6, 2026. Paris has direct Air Seychelles flights on Wednesdays, Fridays, and Sundays.

For travelers without a suitable direct service, the relevant connections are Turkish Airlines via Istanbul, Ethiopian Airlines via Addis Ababa, and Kenya Airways via Nairobi. All international flights arrive at Seychelles International Airport, SEZ, on Mahé.

Decision: start with the closest direct departure airport, then compare total travel time against a one-stop itinerary from your home airport. Do not choose by airline list alone; connection duration and arrival time on Mahé affect the first day of the trip. These schedules can change, so airline verification is required before booking.
facts: Frankfurt: Condor and Discover Airlines direct services.
Zurich: Edelweiss Air direct service; seasonal gap listed May 30–September 6, 2026.
Paris: Air Seychelles direct on Wednesday, Friday, and Sunday.
Connections: Istanbul, Addis Ababa, or Nairobi.

format.intro: The useful European choices are a small set of direct routes plus three established one-stop connections.
format.context: Frankfurt, Zurich, and Paris are the direct European departure points identified in AYOR. The seasonal pattern matters as much as the airline: a direct route may not operate on your dates, while a well-timed one-stop journey can produce a calmer arrival on Mahé. Start with the nearest direct airport, but compare the complete door-to-island journey before deciding. Arrival time matters when a ferry or domestic flight follows.

topics.label: Seychelles ETA
topics.answer:
All visitors must obtain a Seychelles Electronic Travel Authorization before departure. The application is completed online and must be approved before the airline allows boarding. The official portal listed in AYOR is seychelles.govtas.com/en.

Prepare the information before starting: passport details, confirmed accommodation, and onward or return travel. Your accommodation should be registered in the official tourism database and approved for use in an ETA application; unregistered accommodation may create problems.

Decision: complete the ETA after your main accommodation and flights are confirmed, but not at the last minute. Use only the official portal and keep the approval accessible on your phone during check-in and arrival. Entry rules and processing requirements can change, so verify the current instructions on the official government site shortly before travel.
facts: ETA is required for all visitors before departure.
Approval is required before boarding.
Official portal: https://seychelles.govtas.com/en
Accommodation must be registered and suitable for the ETA application.

format.intro: All visitors need an approved Seychelles Electronic Travel Authorization before boarding.
format.context: The application is completed online before travel. Accommodation details matter because the property should be registered in the official tourism database and accepted for the ETA process. An unregistered stay can create avoidable approval problems even when the rest of the journey is confirmed. Complete the application in one sitting after your first stay and onward travel are fixed, and save the confirmation offline as well as in email. Check that names and passport numbers match exactly before submitting, especially when several travelers are included.

topics.label: Currency and payments
topics.answer:
The official currency is the Seychellois Rupee, SCR. AYOR recommends exchanging a small amount at the airport arrivals counters after baggage claim; a passport may be required and commission fees can apply. Visa and Mastercard are widely accepted in hotels, restaurants, supermarkets, car rentals, and tour agencies. Apple Pay or Google Pay may work in some places, but should not be your only method.

Cash is still useful for markets, local takeaway restaurants, small beach bars, and some taxis. Bring one or two bank cards, some EUR or USD cash, and a modest amount of SCR. ATMs exist on Mahé, Praslin, and La Digue, but fees and withdrawal limits can be high.

Decision: use a multi-currency card for larger payments and carry small SCR notes for daily local purchases. If a price is displayed in EUR but charged in SCR, check your bank’s conversion fees.
facts: Official currency: Seychellois Rupee (SCR).
Cards are common in formal businesses; cash remains useful locally.
ATMs can charge high fees and impose limits.
Recommended setup: 1–2 cards, some EUR/USD, and some SCR.

topics.label: Mobile internet
topics.answer:
AYOR recommends an eSIM as the simplest mobile internet setup, with Airalo listed as the preferred provider. Install it before departure so it can activate quickly on arrival. An eSIM avoids airport registration and a physical SIM swap, and is described as working across Mahé, Praslin, and La Digue.

Your phone must support eSIM and be unlocked. A physical SIM remains available at airport arrivals if your device is not compatible. Internet access in Seychelles is relatively expensive, so choose a data allowance based on navigation, messaging, and essential travel use rather than constant streaming.

Decision: verify eSIM compatibility before buying, install the plan while you still have reliable home Wi-Fi, and keep the QR code or activation details available offline. If your phone is locked or not compatible, use the airport physical-SIM option.
facts: Recommended option: eSIM.
Provider listed: Airalo.
Works on Mahé, Praslin, and La Digue.
Requires an unlocked, eSIM-compatible phone.

topics.label: Public buses on Mahé
topics.answer:
Mahé’s public bus network is operated by SPTC and covers the island through routes centered on Victoria. AYOR lists operating hours of roughly 05:30–20:30, with typical frequencies of 15–30 minutes. Delays are common, signage can be minimal, and passengers should signal the driver at the stop.

A rechargeable transport card is required; cash and ordinary bank cards are generally not accepted. For navigation, use the SPTC app or maps, Google Maps, or Maps.me. Add a 30–60 minute buffer and avoid peak periods around 08:00–09:00 and 16:00–18:00 when possible.

Decision: buses are suitable for travelers staying in well-connected areas such as Beau Vallon and for flexible days without tight reservations. They are less suitable for remote accommodation, multiple beach stops, heavy luggage, or time-critical airport and ferry transfers.
facts: Operator: SPTC.
Approximate schedule: 05:30–20:30.
Rechargeable transport card required.
Add 30–60 minutes for delays.

topics.label: Taxis on Mahé
topics.answer:
Mahé does not have Uber, Bolt, or a comparable ride-hailing app. Taxis are operated by private licensed drivers and are usually arranged through a hotel, by WhatsApp, or at official taxi stands at the airport and in Victoria.

AYOR lists indicative fares of 300–400 SCR from the airport to Victoria and 400–600 SCR from the airport to Beau Vallon. Short rides may be around 100–200 SCR. Taxis generally do not use meters, so agree the total price before the journey begins and confirm whether waiting time, luggage, or extra stops are included.

Decision: taxis are practical for airport transfers, evenings, and occasional point-to-point trips. If you expect several full-day journeys around Mahé, compare the combined taxi cost with a small rental car or a prearranged transfer. For an early departure or remote accommodation, book the return journey in advance.
facts: No Uber or Bolt.
Arrange through hotels, WhatsApp, airport, or Victoria taxi stands.
Airport–Victoria: 300–400 SCR.
Airport–Beau Vallon: 400–600 SCR.
No meters; agree the price first.

If your accommodation is remote or your departure is early, arrange the return journey rather than relying on finding a passing taxi. Save the driver’s WhatsApp number when the first journey is reliable; this can make later pickups much simpler. Confirm the pickup time in writing.

topics.label: Car rental
topics.answer:
A rental car is most useful on Mahé when your accommodation is outside a bus-friendly area or you want to combine remote beaches and viewpoints in one day. Driving is on the left, cars are right-hand drive, and roads can be narrow, mountainous, and sharply curved.

AYOR recommends small cars such as a Toyota Vitz, Passo, Aqua, Hyundai i10, or i20. Large SUVs and low sports cars are less suitable. Parking is mostly free, fuel is expensive, and stations are limited. Typical requirements include a passport, driving licence, and a minimum age around 21–23 depending on the company. A small car is listed at roughly 45–70 EUR per day.

Decision: consider a partial rental for selected exploration days rather than the entire stay. Allow an easy first drive while you adjust to the road layout and left-side traffic.
facts: Drive on the left; right-hand steering.
Roads are narrow, steep, and mountainous.
Small cars are recommended.
Indicative rental: 45–70 EUR per day.

Rent a small car for two or three exploration days.
Use those days for south Mahé, viewpoints, and remote beaches.

practical_options.heading: Choose the car carefully
items: Choose a Toyota Vitz, Passo, Aqua, Hyundai i10, or i20.
Avoid large SUVs on narrow mountain roads.

relevant_facts: Driving is on the left.
Parking is mostly free.
Fuel is expensive.
A small car typically costs about 45–70 EUR per day.
designer_response.important: Allow an easy first drive while you adjust to left-side traffic and steep roads.

topics.label: Airport transfers
topics.answer:
AYOR separates transfers into four practical options: private transfer, shared shuttle, hotel transfer, and taxi on arrival. A private airport-route transfer is listed at approximately 35–60 EUR, while a shared shuttle is listed at roughly 10–15 EUR per person. Hotel transfers are described as the most convenient option, particularly when arrival is late or the accommodation is remote.

Decision: use a shared shuttle when price matters and your timing is flexible. Choose a hotel or private transfer when you want a confirmed driver, have substantial luggage, or are staying outside the main transport areas. A taxi from the official airport queue works well for straightforward arrivals, but agree the total fare before leaving.

Transfer prices and availability change, so confirm the current quote and whether it includes waiting time, luggage, and late-arrival fees.
facts: Private airport transfer: approximately 35–60 EUR.
Shared shuttle: approximately 10–15 EUR per person.
Hotel transfer: most convenient.
Taxi: available on arrival.

topics.label: Inter-island ferries
topics.answer:
The main inter-island ferry route is Cat Cocos from Mahé to Praslin, followed by Cat Rose from Praslin to La Digue. AYOR gives a Mahé–Praslin journey time of about 1 hour 15 minutes to 1 hour 45 minutes, with indicative fares of 60–100 EUR. Praslin–La Digue takes about 15–20 minutes and is listed at 15–25 EUR.

Check in around one hour before departure. Sea conditions can be rough, especially during the southeast trade-wind season from June to September. Travelers prone to seasickness should prepare accordingly and may prefer an earlier crossing.

Decision: build the itinerary in a clean Mahé–Praslin–La Digue sequence where possible. Do not pair a weather-sensitive ferry with a tight international flight; keep a generous buffer or spend the final night on Mahé. Verify current schedules and fares before travel.
facts: Cat Cocos: Mahé–Praslin.
Cat Rose: Praslin–La Digue.
Mahé–Praslin: 1h15–1h45; 60–100 EUR.
Praslin–La Digue: 15–20 min; 15–25 EUR.
Check in one hour before.

topics.label: Beaches and tides
topics.answer:
Mahé normally has two high and two low tides each day, with enough variation to change water depth, swimming space, and the character of shallow beaches. Check a current tide forecast for the exact day rather than relying on a general recommendation.

AYOR lists Beau Vallon, Anse Royale, Anse Soleil, and Anse Takamaka among the better swimming choices. Anse Royale is known for a calm lagoon, while Anse Soleil is noted for snorkeling. Anse Intendance and Grand Anse can have stronger waves and ocean conditions. Anse Major requires hiking access.

Decision: choose the beach according to season, wind direction, and the conditions you see on arrival. During June–September, south and southeast beaches can be rougher while west-coast beaches are often calmer. Never treat a visually calm beach as automatically safe; currents and sudden waves remain the main risks.
facts: Two high and two low tides daily.
Swimming choices: Beau Vallon, Anse Royale, Anse Soleil, Anse Takamaka.
Rougher beaches can include Anse Intendance and Grand Anse.
June–September often brings rougher conditions on south and southeast coasts.

topics.label: Fruit and markets
topics.answer:
For fruit, focus on Victoria Market and local produce rather than the wider food system. AYOR lists mango, papaya, banana, and passion fruit as common fruit options. Victoria Market is the central reference point and is best visited in the morning; the market’s strongest fresh-fish window is listed as 06:30–09:00, which is also a practical time to see produce before the day becomes busier.

Availability is seasonal, so ask vendors what is local and good that week rather than arriving with a fixed list. Carry some small SCR notes because cash is often useful at markets and smaller local sellers.

Decision: visit Victoria Market early, buy what is visibly abundant and locally grown, and use roadside stands as a secondary option while travelling around Mahé. Imported produce is likely to be more expensive and less representative of the islands.
facts: Main market reference: Victoria Market.
Fruit listed: mango, papaya, banana, passion fruit.
Morning is the practical market window.
Cash is often useful at markets.

format.intro: For fruit, start with Victoria Market, but do not rely only on supermarkets or the central market.
format.context: Availability is seasonal, so the best purchase depends on what was grown locally that week. Imported fruit is generally less representative and may cost more. The early market hours also make it easier to combine fruit shopping with the fresh-fish section before Victoria becomes busier. Look for produce that is abundant rather than perfectly uniform, and ask the seller what is ready to eat that day versus what should ripen at your accommodation. Buy small quantities first and return if needed.

topics.label: Food and takeaway
topics.answer:
Local takeaway is the main budget-friendly food system in AYOR, usually offering rice, fish, and curry dishes for around 60–120 SCR per meal. Fish is described as the best-value protein. Mid-range restaurants are listed at roughly 200–500 SCR per person, while luxury dining can reach 700–2000+ SCR per person.

For groceries, STC Hypermarket is the main supermarket reference and SPAR Eden Plaza is the premium option. Food is expensive because many products are imported, and cash is often required at smaller takeaways and local businesses.

Decision: use a mixed approach rather than restaurants for every meal. A practical pattern is breakfast at the accommodation, takeaway for lunch, and either a restaurant or supermarket dinner. This keeps costs controlled while still allowing selected dining experiences. Prices are indicative and should be checked locally.
facts: Takeaway meal: approximately 60–120 SCR.
Mid-range restaurant: approximately 200–500 SCR per person.
STC Hypermarket: main supermarket.
SPAR Eden Plaza: premium supermarket.
Fish is the best-value protein.

topics.label: Weather and seasons
topics.answer:
Seychelles has a tropical climate throughout the year, with typical daytime temperatures of 27–31°C, nights around 24–27°C, and ocean temperatures of 26–30°C. Rain is possible in every month and often arrives in short tropical bursts rather than ruining an entire day.

December–March is hotter and more humid, with a higher chance of rain but calmer water on many beaches. April–May and October–November are transition periods with less wind, calmer seas, and good underwater visibility. June–September is cooler and less humid but windier, with rougher seas on exposed south and southeast coasts; it is often better for hiking.

Decision: choose April–May or October–November for swimming, snorkeling, diving, and island hopping. Choose June–September for hiking and lower humidity, while planning beaches more carefully. Weather can differ significantly across Mahé on the same day.
facts: Day: 27–31°C; night: 24–27°C; ocean: 26–30°C.
April–May and October–November: calmer transition periods.
June–September: windier, cooler, rougher on exposed coasts.
Rain can occur year-round and often changes quickly.

topics.label: Where to stay on Mahé
topics.answer:
Beau Vallon is AYOR’s strongest all-round recommendation for first-time visitors and travelers without a car. It has the most developed infrastructure, beach access, restaurants, takeaways, and useful bus connections, though it is busier and more tourist-oriented.

Victoria works best for transit stays and access to services, but not for a beach-focused holiday. Eden Island suits travelers who prefer a modern, secure marina environment with restaurants and cafés, although it feels less local. Anse Royale offers a quieter southeast-coast atmosphere, lagoon swimming, and snorkeling, but has fewer restaurants and is harder without a car. South Mahé suits privacy, nature, and villas, with a rental car strongly recommended.

Decision: choose Beau Vallon for ease, Eden Island or southern villas for luxury, Anse Royale for a calmer local base, and South Mahé for nature and privacy.
facts: Best without a car and for first visits: Beau Vallon.
Best modern luxury area: Eden Island.
Best calm local atmosphere: Anse Royale.
Best nature and privacy: South Mahé, with a car recommended.

topics.label: Budget
topics.answer:
Seychelles is not a low-cost destination because many products are imported and island logistics and fuel add expense. AYOR estimates budget travel at roughly 70–120 EUR per person per day, using a guesthouse or apartment, public buses, and local takeaway food. Mid-range travel is estimated at 150–300 EUR per day, with a good hotel or apartment, some taxis, and a partial car rental. Luxury travel is listed at approximately 400–1500+ EUR per day.

Useful reference costs include takeaway meals at 60–120 SCR, mid-range restaurants at 200–500 SCR per person, airport–Beau Vallon taxis at 400–600 SCR, and small rental cars at 45–70 EUR per day.

Decision: control the budget through accommodation type, local food, and selective transport. A self-catering stay, buses, takeaways, and a short rather than full-trip car rental create the largest savings.
facts: Budget: 70–120 EUR per person/day.
Mid-range: 150–300 EUR/day.
Luxury: 400–1500+ EUR/day.
Small car: 45–70 EUR/day.
Takeaway: 60–120 SCR.

topics.label: Safety
topics.answer:
Seychelles is described in AYOR as one of the safer destinations in Africa and the Indian Ocean, with violent crime against tourists considered rare. The more important practical risks are ocean conditions, strong UV, slippery trails, narrow roads, and leaving valuables unattended.

Strong currents, sudden waves, and slippery rocks require caution, especially at wilder beaches such as Anse Intendance and Grand Anse. Use SPF 50+, stay hydrated, and avoid the strongest sun between 11:00 and 15:00. Trails can become slippery after rain, so use proper shoes and carry water. Mosquitoes are more active after rain, near vegetation, and at sunset.

Decision: observe local beach conditions, avoid swimming alone on wild beaches, secure valuables, and bring personal medication. Pharmacies are available on Mahé and Praslin. Tap water is generally considered safe in many areas, though many visitors prefer bottled water.
facts: Violent crime against tourists is rare.
Main risks: ocean conditions, sun, slippery trails, and driving.
Use SPF 50+ and avoid peak sun 11:00–15:00.
Bring personal medication.

format.intro: Seychelles is described in AYOR as one of the safer destinations in Africa and the Indian Ocean, with violent crime against tourists considered rare. The more important practical risks are ocean conditions, strong UV, slippery trails, narrow roads, and leaving valuables unattended.
format.context: Strong currents, sudden waves, and slippery rocks require caution, especially at wilder beaches such as Anse Intendance and Grand Anse. Use SPF 50+, stay hydrated, and avoid the strongest sun between 11:00 and 15:00. Trails can become slippery after rain, so use proper shoes and carry water. Mosquitoes are more active after rain, near vegetation, and at sunset.

sections.heading: Key details
items: Violent crime against tourists is rare.
Main risks: ocean conditions, sun, slippery trails, and driving.
Use SPF 50+ and avoid peak sun 11:00–15:00.
Bring personal medication.

format.important: Decision: observe local beach conditions, avoid swimming alone on wild beaches, secure valuables, and bring personal medication. Pharmacies are available on Mahé and Praslin. Tap water is generally considered safe in many areas, though many visitors prefer bottled water.

general_facts: Violent crime against tourists is rare.
Main risks: ocean conditions, sun, slippery trails, and driving.
Use SPF 50+ and avoid peak sun 11:00–15:00.
Bring personal medication.
ayor_notes: The ocean is usually the practical risk, not crime. Watch the water before entering and treat local warnings more seriously than how calm a beach looks.

designer_response.recommendation: Treat ocean conditions, sun, and road awareness as the main practical safety priorities rather than worrying excessively about crime.

Mosquitoes are more active after rain, near vegetation, and at sunset.
